levels.name: Touched
levels.mechanicalConsequence: Advantage on Perception checks for Fray phenomena; disadvantage on same check type in bright/crowded/normal environments

levels.name: Marked
levels.mechanicalConsequence: Lose proficiency in one Knowledge skill (History, Arcana, Nature, or Religion — player's choice); gain proficiency in Survival

levels.name: Altered
levels.mechanicalConsequence: Always know the exact time; cannot be surprised; disadvantage on Initiative rolls

levels.name: Changed
levels.mechanicalConsequence: Resistance to cold damage; disadvantage on Persuasion checks with non-Fray-touched creatures; advantage with Fray-touched

levels.name: Resonant
levels.mechanicalConsequence: Detect Magic at will (Threadlines and Fray activity only, 120 ft range); immune to Frayed zone environmental damage and exposure saves

levels.name: Sighted
levels.mechanicalConsequence: True Seeing (weave structure only); DC 15 Constitution save when entering new location or be stunned until end of next turn
